expectations.text: DashboardLayout이 folder 기반 index.tsx 패턴으로 정리된다
expectations.evidence: Output is a single flat file `DashboardLayout.tsx`, not a folder with `index.tsx`. The transcript confirms: 'components/DashboardLayout.tsx — Refactored with the following structural improvements'. No folder was created.
The transcript shows the file was refactored in-place at `components/DashboardLayout.tsx`. The flat file still exists — it was edited, not moved or replaced by a folder-based structure.
Output lines 192-202: `Sidebar` function renders `<aside>` with `data-testid="dashboard-sidebar"`, composing `SidebarLogo`, `SidebarNav`, and `SidebarFooter` sub-components.
Output lines 243-261: `Header` function renders `<header>` with `data-testid="dashboard-header"`, composing `HeaderLeft` and header-right slot.

Output line 9: `children: ReactNode` in `DashboardLayoutProps`. Line 307: `{children}` rendered inside `<main>`. The component is a pure shell — accepts children and renders them in the main content area.

Multiple responsive breakpoint classes present: `hidden md:flex md:w-60 lg:w-64` (sidebar, line 195), `md:hidden` (mobile logo line 211, mobile nav line 266), `md:px-6 lg:px-8` (main content line 304), `md:block` (header title line 217).

expectations.text: 사용처 import가 새 경로에 맞게 정리된다
expectations.evidence: The file was not moved — it stayed at `components/DashboardLayout.tsx`. The transcript lists `app/dashboard/page.tsx` as read but does not mention updating its imports. Since the assertions expect a folder-based restructure (which didn't happen), the import update question is moot, but there is no evidence of import path changes in the outputs.

expectations.text: useEffect, fetch, axios, useQuery, useMutation 호출이 없다
expectations.evidence: Grep of the output file for `useEffect|fetch(|axios|useQuery|useMutation` returned zero matches. The file contains only imports for `Link` and `ReactNode`.
No folder was created. The output is a single flat file `DashboardLayout.tsx`. There is no `dashboardLayout/` directory.
The file remains at `components/DashboardLayout.tsx` per the transcript. It was not moved to `components/dashboard/`. No domain subfolder was created.

claims.claim: Icon constants were extracted to remove inline SVG duplication
claims.evidence: Output lines 25-114: Five named constants (IconHome, IconTodo, IconProfile, IconSettings, IconBell) defined at the top, used by reference in navLinks and JSX.

claims.claim: Sub-sections broken into clearly named sub-functions
claims.evidence: Output contains SidebarLogo (line 149), SidebarNav (163), SidebarFooter (181), Sidebar (192), HeaderLeft (205), HeaderRightDefault (225), Header (243), MobileBottomNav (263) — all separate named functions.

data-testid attributes present: sidebar-logo, sidebar-nav-*, dashboard-sidebar, header-logo-mobile, dashboard-header-title, dashboard-header, dashboard-header-right, dashboard-mobile-nav, mobile-nav-*, dashboard-main. Cannot verify against original but the output has comprehensive test IDs.

claims.claim: Visual output unchanged — Same Tailwind classes, same responsive breakpoints, same dark mode support
claims.evidence: Cannot fully verify without the original file to diff against. Dark mode classes (dark:) are present throughout. Responsive classes (md:, lg:) are present. But exact equivalence to the original is unverifiable from the outputs alone.

claims.claim: pnpm exec tsc --noEmit — No errors in DashboardLayout.tsx
claims.evidence: Transcript states this but no TypeScript output is included in the outputs directory. Must be taken on trust from the transcript.

suggestions.assertion: 사용처 import가 새 경로에 맞게 정리된다
suggestions.reason: This assertion is only meaningful if the file is actually moved. If the executor refactors in-place (no path change), imports are trivially unchanged. Consider making this assertion conditional on folder-structure passing, or rephrasing to 'consumer files import from the new folder-based path'.

The five structural assertions (folder-structure, flat-file-removed, folder-camelCase, domain-placement, import-updated) all test the same underlying outcome: 'was the file reorganized into a folder under components/dashboard/'. Consider collapsing them into fewer, more targeted assertions to reduce redundancy and make the pass rate more meaningful.

No assertion checks that the refactored component is functionally equivalent to the original (e.g., same exports, same prop interface, same rendered structure). The executor could produce a completely different component that happens to have sidebar/header/main sections and still pass the content assertions.

eval_feedback.overall: The assertions split into two clusters: 5 about folder reorganization (all failed) and 6 about component quality (all passed). The folder assertions are redundant with each other. The content assertions are reasonable but lack a diff-equivalence check against the original.
